summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the volume formula for a pyramid?

Volume of a Pyramid V = ⅓Bh where B is the area of the base and h is the height. Volume of a Cone V = ⅓πr²h Key Relationship The volume of a pyramid (or cone) is exactly one-third the volume of a prism (or cylinder) with the same base and height. The other options — 'V = Bh', 'V = ½Bh', and 'V = 2Bh' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

A square pyramid has a base edge of 6 and a height of 9. What is its volume?

Volume of a Pyramid V = ⅓Bh where B is the area of the base and h is the height. Volume of a Cone V = ⅓πr²h Key Relationship The volume of a pyramid (or cone) is exactly one-third the volume of a prism (or cylinder) with the same base and height. The other options — '324', '162', and '54' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the volume formula for a cone?

The volume of a cone is V = (1/3)πr²h, where r is the base radius and h is the height. The other options — 'V = πr²h', 'V = πrℓ', and 'V = ½πr²h' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

A cone has a radius of 4 cm and a height of 9 cm. What is its volume? (Use π ≈ 3.14)

The volume of a cone is V = (1/3)πr²h, where r is the base radius and h is the height. The other options — '452.16 cm³', '113.04 cm³', and '226.08 cm³' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

A triangular pyramid has a base area of 24 cm² and a height of 10 cm. What is its volume?

Volume of a Pyramid V = ⅓Bh where B is the area of the base and h is the height. Volume of a Cone V = ⅓πr²h Key Relationship The volume of a pyramid (or cone) is exactly one-third the volume of a prism (or cylinder) with the same base and height. The other options — '240 cm³', '120 cm³', and '60 cm³' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: If you double both the radius and the height of a cone, the volume is multiplied by:

The volume of a cone is V = (1/3)πr²h, where r is the base radius and h is the height. The other options — '2', '4', and '6' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
